headline: Johnson & Johnson to Host Investor Conference Call on First-Quarter Results
summary: NEW BRUNSWICK, N.J., March 02, 2026--Johnson & Johnson (NYSE: JNJ) will host a conference call for investors at 8:30 a.m. (Eastern Time) on Tuesday, April 14th to review first-quarter results. Joaquin Duato, Chairman and Chief Executive Officer, Joseph J. Wolk, Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer and Darren Snellgrove, Vice President, Investor Relations will host the call. headline: Johnson And Johnson Weighs Drug Breakthroughs Against Possible DePuy Synthes Sale
summary: Johnson & Johnson (NYSE:JNJ) reported encouraging early clinical results for its prostate cancer therapy pasritamig and is preparing for Phase 3 trials. The company has submitted nipocalimab to the FDA as a potential first-in-class treatment for warm autoimmune hemolytic anemia. Reports indicate Johnson & Johnson is exploring options for its DePuy Synthes orthopedics unit, including a possible sale that could exceed US$20b. For you as an investor, these updates touch both sides of Johnson &...

headline: 4 Large-Cap Pharma Stocks to Watch as Industry Recovery Gains Steam
summary: In the Large-Cap Pharmaceuticals industry, Lilly, J&J, BAYRY and Sanofi are worth retaining as the industry shows some recovery.

headline: AbbVie Says 55% Of Crohn's Patients Reached Remission With Skyrizi, Bolsters Competitive Positioning Against Johnson & Johnson
summary: AbbVie says 55% of Crohn's patients reached remission in a late-stage study, boosting Skyrizi's outlook amid market competition.

headline: Zymeworks Provides Corporate Update and Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Financial Results
summary: Supplemental Biologics License Application for Ziihera® (zanidatamab-hrii) to be completed by our partner Jazz in first-line HER2-positive (HER2+) gastroesophageal adenocarcinoma (GEA) during 1Q 2026 in the U.S. with potential launch in 2H 2026Up to $440.0 million in milestone payments eligible to be earned related to regulatory approvals of Ziihera in GEA in the U.S., Europe, Japan, and ChinaAdditional clinical data for pasritamig in prostate cancer presented at ASCO-GU in February 2026 by John
